title.en: I have to disagree! 나는 동의할 수 없다! 난 반대야!
title.ko: 나는 동의할 수 없다! 난 반대야!

description.en: When it is hard to agree with someone's opinion! Practice expressing your thoughts clearly with 나는 동의할 수 없다 and 난 반대야.
description.ko: 상대방의 의견에 동의하기 어려울 때! 나는 동의할 수 없다와 난 반대야를 사용해 내 생각을 분명하게 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Disagreement. Opposition. Opinion. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 오늘 날씨도 정말 좋은데 우리 퇴근하고 같이 등산하러 갈까요?
content.translation: John. the weather is so nice today. Shall we go hiking together after work?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 산 정상에서 먹는 김밥이 진짜 최고거든요!
content.translation: Kimbap at the top of the mountain is the best!

content.speaker: John
content.text: What? Hiking? Mina. I'm sorry. but 나는 동의할 수 없어요. This weather is perfect for watching movies at home with the AC on.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니. 이렇게 맑은 날에 집에만 있는다고요? 전 정말 이해가 안 가네요!
content.translation: What? Staying home on such a clear day? I really don't understand!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미안하지만 난 반대야. 산은 밑에서 멀리 바라보는 게 제일 아름다운 법이죠.
content.translation: I'm sorry. but I'm against it. Mountains are most beautiful when viewed from a distance at the bottom.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 역시 존이랑 저는 취향이 참 다르네요. 정말 아쉬워요!
content.translation: As expected. you and I have very different tastes. John. That's too bad!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나는 동의할 수 없다. This sounds very firm.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 상대방의 의견에 대해 내 입장이 완전히 다를 때 아주 분명하게 말할 수 있는 표현이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It is an expression you can use to state your position very clearly when it is completely different from the other person's opinion.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나는 동의할 수 없다.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 그렇게 생각하지 않는다 라는 말은 상대방의 말에 정중하게 반대 의견을 낼 때 사용하기 좋아요.
content.translation: And the phrase 'Geu-reot-ge saeng-gak-ha-ji an-neun-da' is good to use when you want to politely disagree with what someone said.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그렇게 생각하지 않는다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about when I want to say "I'm against it."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 난 반대야 라고 해보세요. 어떤 계획이나 제안에 찬성하지 않는다는 뜻을 확실하게 전달할 수 있어요.
content.translation: In that case. try saying 'Nan ban-dae-ya'. You can clearly convey that you do not agree with a certain plan or suggestion.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try another situation. Imagine we are deciding what to eat for dinner. and I suggest mint chocolate pizza.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 지금 뭐라고 했어요? 민트 초코 피자요?
content.translation: John. what did you just say? Mint chocolate pizza?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yeah! It's sweet. cool. and refreshing. It's the perfect dinner!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 나는 동의할 수 없다! 피자는 짭짤하고 고소해야죠. 민트 초코라니 정말 말도 안 돼요.
content.translation: I have to disagree! Pizza should be salty and savory. Mint chocolate is just nonsense.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh. come on! You have to try new things. Mina.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 미안하지만 이번 제안은 난 반대야. 전 그냥 평범한 페퍼로니 피자를 먹을래요.
content.translation: I'm sorry. but I'm against this suggestion. I'll just eat a regular pepperoni pizza.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. okay. 그렇게 생각하지 않는다 면 어쩔 수 없죠. 다음번에 도전해 봐요.
content.translation: Okay. okay. If you don't think so. I can't help it. Let's try it next time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's okay to have different opinions. That's what makes conversations interesting.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 서로 생각이 다를 때 오늘 배운 표현들로 내 의견을 솔직하고 분명하게 말해 보세요!
content.translation: Right. When you have different thoughts. try to speak your mind honestly and clearly with the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "disagree" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나는 동의할 수 없다! I have to disagree!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그렇게 생각하지 않는다! I don't think so!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 난 반대야! I'm against it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 좀 아닌 것 같아! I don't think that's right!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 내 생각은 달라! I have a different opinion!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 누군가와 의견이 다르더라도 당황하지 말고 내 생각을 당당하게 말해 보세요!
content.translation: Even if you disagree with someone today. don't be flustered and speak your mind confidently!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 저는 그 의견에 동의해요. (I agree with that opinion.)
이번 계획에 반대하는 사람이 많아요. (Many people are against this plan.)
각자의 의견을 자유롭게 말씀해 주세요. (Please feel free to share your opinions.)
사람마다 취향이 다를 수 있죠. (Tastes can differ from person to person.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be polite. To be courteous
vocabulary.example: 존은 항상 정중하게 말해서 기분이 좋아요. (It feels good because John always speaks politely.)
자신의 생각을 당당하게 표현하는 게 중요해요. (It's important to express your thoughts confidently.)
